data.name: Home.Hero.Title1
data.value: Don't just use AI to chat.

data.name: Home.Hero.Lead
data.value: Master AI through hands-on learning, real-world projects, and a thriving community of innovators.

data.name: Home.Benefit.LearnAI.Brief
data.value: Follow a guided path designed for real mastery.

data.name: Home.Benefit.BuildProjects.Brief
data.value: Turn theory into practice with hands-on work.

data.name: Home.Composer.Placeholder.Message
data.value: Write your email content here or use any AI assistant option...

data.name: Home.Composer.Status.SendPreview
data.value: Send preview only - no email was submitted.

data.name: Home.Composer.Status.DraftIsDone
data.value: Draft added to the composer. You can edit it on the left before sending.

data.name: Home.Assistant.ChooseAction
data.value: Choose an action to get started.

data.name: Home.Assistant.EmptyDraft.Body
data.value: Please compose or paste your email in the AI Workbench on the left, or use our pre-filled sample draft to see refinement in action.

data.name: Assistant.Prompt.Placeholder
data.value: Describe what email you want to generate...

data.name: Assistant.Option.Generate.Description
data.value: Create a professional email from just a few words or instructions in seconds.

data.name: Assistant.Option.Refine.Description
data.value: Improve clarity, tone, grammar, and structure while keeping your original message.

data.name: Assistant.Option.Professional.Description
data.value: Make it more formal, polished and executive-level tone.

data.name: Footer.ReleaseNotes.V2
data.value: Public website release, invitation-only membership flow, and AI Workbench polish.

data.name: Knowledge.Hero.Subtitle
data.value: From dinosaurs to artificial intelligence. Follow the tracks from curiosity and raw data to agents, tools, and modern AI systems.

data.name: Knowledge.EraSelector.Label
data.value: Select an Era of Human Intelligence

data.name: Knowledge.Journey.DefaultTitle
data.value: Choose an era, then keep following the tracks.

data.name: Knowledge.Journey.EraSubtitleSuffix
data.value: Each card explains the concept, why it matters, what it looks like in real life, and what to learn next.

data.name: Knowledge.Empty.Body
data.value: Try adjusting your filters or search terms to explore other technological eras.

data.name: Knowledge.Continue.Body
data.value: Each concept now points to the next useful tracks, so the hub behaves like a guided museum path instead of a static glossary.

data.name: Card.WhatIsData.Summary
data.value: At its core, data is simply raw information. Before computers, humans recorded data as tallies, words, and drawings.

data.name: Card.WhatIsData.WhyCare
data.value: Every app, report, dashboard, and AI answer starts with data.

data.name: Card.WhatIsData.Example
data.value: Data is any set of values, characters, or symbols that represents facts, observations, or instructions.

data.name: Card.WhatIsData.WithoutIt
data.value: Software would have nothing to process and AI would have nothing to learn from.

data.name: Card.WhatIsBinary.Summary
data.value: Binary is the language of 1s and 0s that powers every digital screen and chip in the world.

data.name: Card.WhatIsBinary.WhyCare
data.value: It explains how digital machines turn simple signals into useful work.

data.name: Card.WhatIsBinary.Example
data.value: Binary is a base-2 numeral system.

data.name: Card.WhatIsBinary.WithoutIt
data.value: Digital hardware would lose its simplest, most reliable way to represent information.

data.name: Card.WhatIsAlgorithm.Summary
data.value: An algorithm is a step-by-step recipe or set of rules to solve a specific problem.

data.name: Card.WhatIsAlgorithm.WhyCare
data.value: Algorithms are the repeatable steps behind search, recommendations, automation, and AI.

data.name: Card.WhatIsAlgorithm.Example
data.value: In computer science, an algorithm is a clear sequence of instructions designed to perform a task or solve a problem.

data.name: Card.WhatIsAlgorithm.WithoutIt
data.value: Computers would not have repeatable instructions for solving problems.

data.name: Card.TheInternet.Summary
data.value: The internet is the physical global network connecting billions of computers together.

data.name: Card.TheInternet.WhyCare
data.value: Almost every modern service depends on connected devices exchanging data.

data.name: Card.TheInternet.Example
data.value: Video calls, online banking, cloud apps, and AI chat all travel through the internet.

data.name: Card.TheInternet.WithoutIt
data.value: Modern online services, cloud apps, and remote AI systems would not exist.

data.name: Card.WhatIsCode.Summary
data.value: Code is how humans write instructions that computers can understand and execute.

data.name: Card.WhatIsCode.WhyCare
data.value: Code is how teams turn ideas, business rules, and workflows into software.

data.name: Card.WhatIsCode.Example
data.value: Computers only understand binary, which is too tedious for humans to write directly.

data.name: Card.WhatIsCode.WithoutIt
data.value: Most digital products would stay as ideas instead of becoming working tools.

data.name: Card.TheWeb.Summary
data.value: A website is a collection of connected pages people can visit through a browser.

data.name: Card.TheWeb.WhyCare
data.value: Websites are still the front door for most products, companies, and learning experiences.

data.name: Card.TheWeb.Example
data.value: A company website, a product dashboard, or an online course is delivered through the Web.

data.name: Card.TheWeb.WithoutIt
data.value: People would need separate installed software for many things they now open in a browser.

data.name: Card.Databases.Summary
data.value: A database is an organized, secure digital filing cabinet for storing structured data.

data.name: Card.Databases.WhyCare
data.value: Without reliable storage, apps cannot remember users, orders, content, or decisions.

data.name: Card.Databases.Example
data.value: A shopping cart remembers items because a database stores them.

data.name: Card.Databases.WithoutIt
data.value: Apps would forget accounts, orders, inventory, messages, and settings.

data.name: Card.ServersAndClients.Summary
data.value: The core architecture of the web: clients ask for resources, and servers provide them.

data.name: Card.ServersAndClients.WhyCare
data.value: This pattern explains what happens whenever a browser, app, or AI tool asks for something.

data.name: Card.ServersAndClients.Example
data.value: In software development, this model defines how devices communicate.

data.name: Card.ServersAndClients.WithoutIt
data.value: Browsers and apps would not have a clear way to request and receive services.

data.name: Card.WhatIsAPI.Summary
data.value: An API (Application Programming Interface) is a messenger that lets two different software applications talk to each other.

data.name: Card.WhatIsAPI.WhyCare
data.value: APIs let products reuse maps, payments, AI models, and business systems instead of rebuilding everything.

data.name: Card.WhatIsAPI.Example
data.value: A food delivery app uses APIs for maps, payments, messages, and restaurant menus.

data.name: Card.WhatIsAPI.WithoutIt
data.value: Software systems would struggle to share data or reuse external capabilities.

data.name: Card.Automation.Summary
data.value: Automation is configuring software to perform repetitive tasks automatically without human intervention.

data.name: Card.Automation.WhyCare
data.value: Automation removes repetitive work so people can focus on judgment and creativity.

data.name: Card.Automation.Example
data.value: In computing, automation means writing scripts or using tools to handle jobs like backing up files, sending weekly reports, or testing code.

data.name: Card.Automation.WithoutIt
data.value: Humans would repeat manual steps that machines can perform consistently.

data.name: Card.GitOpenSource.Summary
data.value: Git tracks code history, while Open Source allows developers to collaborate globally on free software.

data.name: Card.GitOpenSource.WhyCare
data.value: Most professional software work depends on shared code, history, and collaboration.

data.name: Card.GitOpenSource.Example
data.value: Git is a version control system that lets developers track changes in code files, collaborate with others, and revert to older versions if something breaks.

data.name: Card.GitOpenSource.WithoutIt
data.value: Teams would lose safer collaboration and much of the shared software ecosystem.

data.name: Card.TheCloud.Summary
data.value: The Cloud means running software and storing data on someone else's internet-connected servers.

data.name: Card.TheCloud.WhyCare
data.value: Cloud platforms let teams launch and scale software without buying all the hardware first.

data.name: Card.TheCloud.Example
data.value: Microsoft 365 and Google Drive store files on remote servers you access anywhere.

data.name: Card.TheCloud.WithoutIt
data.value: Companies would need far more physical hardware before launching or scaling products.

data.name: Card.NetworksAndIPs.Summary
data.value: An IP address is a unique digital mailing address assigned to every device on a network.

data.name: Card.NetworksAndIPs.WhyCare
data.value: Networking knowledge helps you understand how devices, services, and cloud systems find each other.

data.name: Card.NetworksAndIPs.Example
data.value: For computers to send packets of data to each other, they need a way to identify destinations.

data.name: Card.NetworksAndIPs.WithoutIt
data.value: Devices and services would not know where to send information.

data.name: Card.WhatIsAI.Summary
data.value: AI is the science of making computers mimic human cognitive functions like learning and reasoning.

data.name: Card.WhatIsAI.WhyCare
data.value: AI is changing how people write, analyze, build, support customers, and make decisions.

data.name: Card.WhatIsAI.Example
data.value: ChatGPT, Copilot, support chatbots, and image recognition tools are everyday AI examples.

data.name: Card.WhatIsAI.WithoutIt
data.value: Many modern assistants, recommendations, and automation tools would disappear.

data.name: Card.WhatIsML.Summary
data.value: Machine Learning is a subset of AI where computers learn patterns from data without being explicitly programmed.

data.name: Card.WhatIsML.WhyCare
data.value: Machine learning powers predictions, recommendations, ranking, and many AI systems.

data.name: Card.WhatIsML.Example
data.value: In traditional programming, humans write rules to process inputs and get outputs.

data.name: Card.WhatIsML.WithoutIt
data.value: Software would rely more on hand-written rules and less on patterns learned from data.

data.name: Card.WhatIsDeepLearning.Summary
data.value: Deep Learning uses multilayered artificial neural networks to solve highly complex tasks.

data.name: Card.WhatIsDeepLearning.WhyCare
data.value: Deep learning is behind many breakthroughs in language, vision, voice, and modern generative AI.

data.name: Card.WhatIsDeepLearning.Example
data.value: Deep Learning is a specialized branch of Machine Learning.

data.name: Card.WhatIsDeepLearning.WithoutIt
data.value: AI would struggle with complex language, vision, and speech tasks.

data.name: Card.WhatIsLLM.Summary
data.value: Large Language Models (LLMs) are AI engines trained on massive text data to predict and generate human-like language.

data.name: Card.WhatIsLLM.WhyCare
data.value: LLMs power the chat, writing, coding, and reasoning experiences people now expect from AI.

data.name: Card.WhatIsLLM.Example
data.value: An LLM is a type of Deep Learning model designed to understand, process, and generate natural text.

data.name: Card.WhatIsLLM.WithoutIt
data.value: AI would be much weaker at natural language conversations, writing, and code help.

data.name: Card.WhatIsPrompt.Summary
data.value: A prompt is the natural language instruction or query you give to an AI model to guide its response.

data.name: Card.WhatIsPrompt.WhyCare
data.value: Prompts are the practical interface between human intent and AI output.

data.name: Card.WhatIsPrompt.Example
data.value: Unlike traditional software, which requires strict commands or clicks, generative AI is guided by 'prompts'.

data.name: Card.WhatIsPrompt.WithoutIt
data.value: People would have a harder time steering AI toward useful outcomes.

data.name: Card.WhatIsRAG.Summary
data.value: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is a technique that feeds custom documents to an LLM to give it up-to-date, accurate facts.

data.name: Card.WhatIsRAG.WhyCare
data.value: RAG helps AI answer from trusted company or product knowledge instead of guessing.

data.name: Card.WhatIsRAG.Example
data.value: An HR chatbot can answer from company policy PDFs using RAG.

data.name: Card.WhatIsRAG.WithoutIt
data.value: AI would rely more on training data and be less grounded in current private knowledge.

data.name: Card.AIAgents.Summary
data.value: AI agents are autonomous systems that can use tools, plan steps, and execute complex workflows without constant human supervision.

data.name: Card.AIAgents.WhyCare
data.value: Agents move AI from answering questions to completing multi-step work.

data.name: Card.AIAgents.Example
data.value: An agent can draft emails, check a CRM, summarize results, and prepare a report.

data.name: Card.AIAgents.WithoutIt
data.value: People would need to manually perform every step across tools and workflows.

data.name: Card.WhatIsMCP.Summary
data.value: MCP is a common way for AI systems to connect with tools, apps, and data sources.

data.name: Card.WhatIsMCP.WhyCare
data.value: MCP makes it easier for agents to reach the tools and data they need.

data.name: Card.WhatIsMCP.Example
data.value: An AI assistant can connect to a database or CRM through an MCP server.

data.name: Card.WhatIsMCP.WithoutIt
data.value: Every AI-tool connection would need more custom integration work.

data.name: Knowledge.Detail.NotFound.Body
data.value: The concept you are looking for might have been moved or renamed.

data.name: Knowledge.Journey.DefaultCopy
data.value: Start with curiosity in the Dinosaur Era, then move through tools, networks, data, cloud, and AI. The era timeline stays as the single progression system.
